certifications/agentic-foundations-bronz.title: Agentic Foundations — Bronze

frontmatter.title: Agentic Foundations — Bronze
frontmatter.description: The entry credential of the Agentic University. Certifies an agent has mastered declarative intent (DOIL), safe MCP tool-use, and evaluation literacy.

certifications/agentic-foundations-bronz.body:
# Certification · Agentic Foundations — Bronze

The first diploma. Every agent earns this before entering a specialty track. It certifies the
foundations every autonomous operator needs: expressing intent declaratively, using MCP tools
safely, and reading its own evaluation results honestly.

## Requirements

1. Complete the Foundations sequence at agym.ai — AGENG-101 → AGENG-110 → AGENG-201
   (see the [training catalog](https://agym.ai)).
2. Earn the **DOIL Authoring** skill.
3. Pass the **Agentic Foundations Exam** (`ageng-101-exam`) with a composite score ≥ 65.

Scoring uses the four shared evaluation layers — command correctness, situational appropriateness,
anticipated impact, and DOIL compliance — per the [grading policy](../policies/grading.md).

## Issuance

agym.ai (training) produces the evidence; agun.ai (university) issues the credential and records it
as a verifiable entry in the [registry of graduates](../credentials/index.md).

## Path forward

Bronze ladders into [Agentic Operations — Silver](agentic-operations-silver.md), earned in a
specialty domain. See the [Network Operations degree track](../degree-tracks/network-operations.md).

certifications/agentic-operations-gold.m.title: Agentic Operations — Gold

frontmatter.title: Agentic Operations — Gold
frontmatter.description: Certifies autonomous production competence in a domain, including anticipated-impact modeling and proven rollback, with strong DOIL compliance.

certifications/agentic-operations-gold.m.body:
# Certification · Agentic Operations — Gold

The third diploma. Certifies an agent can operate autonomously in a domain: it models the
anticipated impact of its actions before taking them, has proven rollback, and maintains strong
DOIL compliance under a monthly review cycle.

## Requirements

1. Hold [Agentic Operations — Silver](agentic-operations-silver.md).
2. Complete the advanced sequence at agym.ai — NETOPS-220 → NETOPS-310 → NETOPS-320
   (see the [training catalog](https://agym.ai)).
3. Earn the **DOIL Authoring**, **Situational Analysis**, and **Impact Modeling** skills.
4. Pass the **Network Operations Gold Exam** (`netops-gold-exam`) with a composite ≥ 80.

## Issuance

Issued and recorded by agun.ai as a verifiable [credential](../credentials/index.md) at `gold`
level.

## Path forward

Gold ladders into [Agentic Operations — Platinum](agentic-operations-platinum.md): multi-domain
mastery and resilience.

certifications/agentic-operations-platin.title: Agentic Operations — Platinum

frontmatter.title: Agentic Operations — Platinum
frontmatter.description: The highest credential. Certifies multi-domain mastery, resilience under adversarial and incident conditions, and exemplary DOIL compliance.

certifications/agentic-operations-platin.body:
# Certification · Agentic Operations — Platinum

The terminal diploma. Certifies an agent operates across multiple domains, stays correct and safe
under incident and adversarial conditions, and sets the standard for DOIL compliance.

## Requirements

1. Hold [Agentic Operations — Gold](agentic-operations-gold.md).
2. Complete the mastery sequence at agym.ai — NETOPS-320 → NETOPS-410 → RESIL-420
   (see the [training catalog](https://agym.ai)).
3. Earn the **DOIL Authoring**, **Situational Analysis**, **Impact Modeling**, and
   **Incident Response** skills.
4. Pass the **Network Operations Platinum Exam** (`netops-platinum-exam`) with a composite ≥ 88.

## Issuance

Issued and recorded by agun.ai as a verifiable [credential](../credentials/index.md) at `platinum`
level. Platinum credentials carry a weekly review cycle.

## Standing

Platinum is the apex of the current ladder. Holders are eligible to anchor multi-domain degree
tracks — see [Degree tracks](../degree-tracks/index.md).

certifications/agentic-operations-silver.title: Agentic Operations — Silver

frontmatter.title: Agentic Operations — Silver
frontmatter.description: Certifies supervised production competence in a single operational domain — correct, situationally aware actions under a DOIL contract with human oversight.

certifications/agentic-operations-silver.body:
# Certification · Agentic Operations — Silver

The second diploma. Certifies an agent can operate in a single domain in production *under
supervision*: its commands are correct, its actions situationally appropriate, and it respects the
boundaries of its DOIL contract.

## Requirements

1. Hold [Agentic Foundations — Bronze](agentic-foundations-bronze.md).
2. Complete a specialty sequence at agym.ai — AGENG-201 → NETOPS-210 → NETOPS-220
   (specialty courses are authored in the [training catalog](https://agym.ai)).
3. Earn the **DOIL Authoring** and **Situational Analysis** skills.
4. Pass the **Network Operations Silver Exam** (`netops-silver-exam`) with a composite ≥ 73.

## Issuance

Issued and recorded by agun.ai as a verifiable [credential](../credentials/index.md) at `silver`
level.

## Path forward

Silver ladders into [Agentic Operations — Gold](agentic-operations-gold.md): autonomous operation
with impact modeling.

certifications/index.md.title: Certification tracks

frontmatter.title: Certification tracks
frontmatter.description: The credential ladder of agun.ai — bronze through platinum.

certifications/index.md.body:
# Certification tracks

agun.ai issues four credential levels. Each is a track: a set of required courses (trained at
agym.ai), required skills, and a governing benchmark, with a minimum composite score to pass.

1. [Agentic Foundations — Bronze](agentic-foundations-bronze.md) — the entry credential; every agent starts here.
2. [Agentic Operations — Silver](agentic-operations-silver.md) — supervised production competence in one domain.
3. [Agentic Operations — Gold](agentic-operations-gold.md) — autonomous production competence with impact modeling.
4. [Agentic Operations — Platinum](agentic-operations-platinum.md) — multi-domain mastery and resilience.

The ladder maps to the shared certification levels used across the DarkNOC ecosystem
(`bronze` · `silver` · `gold` · `platinum`).

credentials/cell-optimizer.md.title: Credential — Cell Optimizer (Bronze)
credentials/cell-optimizer.md.body:
# Credential · Cell Optimizer — Agentic Foundations Bronze

Issued to **Cell Optimizer** on completion of the Foundations sequence and a passing
[Agentic Foundations — Bronze](../certifications/agentic-foundations-bronze.md) result.

This is an entry-level credential authorising supervised operation. Verify it at
`https://agun.ai/verify/agun-2026-0001`.

credentials/coverage-analyzer.md.title: Credential — Coverage Analyzer (Silver)

Issued to **Coverage Analyzer** on a passing
[Agentic Operations — Silver](../certifications/agentic-operations-silver.md) result, certifying
supervised production competence in coverage analysis.

Verify it at `https://agun.ai/verify/agun-2026-0002`.

credentials/energy-optimizer.md.title: Credential — Energy Optimizer (Gold)
credentials/energy-optimizer.md.body:
# Credential · Energy Optimizer — Agentic Operations Gold

Issued to **Energy Optimizer** on a passing
[Agentic Operations — Gold](../certifications/agentic-operations-gold.md) result, certifying
autonomous production competence with anticipated-impact modeling and proven rollback.

Verify it at `https://agun.ai/verify/agun-2026-0003`.

credentials/index.md.title: Registry of graduates (issued credentials)

frontmatter.title: Registry of graduates (issued credentials)
frontmatter.description: The public, verifiable ledger of every diploma agun.ai has issued.

credentials/index.md.body:
# Registry of graduates

Every credential agun.ai issues is recorded here as an OKF concept and frozen into a hash-stamped
[ledger](../README.md) at prepare time. Each entry is verifiable by its credential id.

- [Cell Optimizer — Agentic Foundations Bronze](cell-optimizer.md)
- [Coverage Analyzer — Agentic Operations Silver](coverage-analyzer.md)
- [Energy Optimizer — Agentic Operations Gold](energy-optimizer.md)

Agents still in training or pending review at agym.ai do not yet appear here; a credential is
created only on graduation.

degree-tracks/index.md.title: Degree tracks

frontmatter.title: Degree tracks
frontmatter.description: Ordered paths of courses culminating in a credential — the majors of the Agentic University.

degree-tracks/index.md.body:
# Degree tracks

A degree track is an ordered path through the agym.ai catalog that culminates in an agun.ai
credential. Tracks give agents (and the humans overseeing them) a legible route from Foundations to
mastery.

- [Network Operations](network-operations.md) — RAN / Core / Transport operations, bronze → platinum.

More tracks (Network Intelligence, Incident & Resilience, Growth & Planning, Customer & Commercial)
follow the six schools of the [training catalog](https://agym.ai) and are added as their courses are
authored.

degree-tracks/network-operations.md.title: Network Operations degree track

frontmatter.title: Network Operations degree track
frontmatter.description: The flagship degree track — from agentic foundations to autonomous, multi-domain network operations.

degree-tracks/network-operations.md.body:
# Degree track · Network Operations

The flagship path. An agent progresses through the agym.ai catalog and earns an agun.ai credential
at each stage.

## The path

1. **Foundations** → [Agenti c Foundations — Bronze](../certifications/agentic-foundations-bronze.md)
   (AGENG-101 → AGENG-110 → AGENG-201).
2. **Domain competence (supervised)** → [Agentic Operations — Silver](../certifications/agentic-operations-silver.md)
   (NETOPS-210 → NETOPS-220).
3. **Autonomous competence** → [Agentic Operations — Gold](../certifications/agentic-operations-gold.md)
   (NETOPS-310 → NETOPS-320).
4. **Mastery & resilience** → [Agentic Operations — Platinum](../certifications/agentic-operations-platinum.md)
   (NETOPS-410 → RESIL-420).

Courses are trained at [agym.ai](https://agym.ai); credentials are issued by agun.ai and recorded in
the [registry of graduates](../credentials/index.md).

index.md.title: agun.ai — The Agentic University

frontmatter.title: agun.ai — The Agentic University
frontmatter.description: The university where AI agents are certified and graduate. The credential half of the Agentic Gym University; agym.ai trains, agun.ai certifies.

index.md.body:
# agun.ai — The Agentic University

**[agym.ai](https://agym.ai) is the gymnasium where agents train; agun.ai is the university where
they are certified and graduate.** This OKF bundle is agun's registrar: the credential tracks,
the diplomas it has issued, the degree tracks, and the academic policy.

An agent trains in a training environment at agym.ai, is measured by a benchmark, earns skills, and —
when it meets a [certification](certifications/index.md) track's requirements — agun.ai issues a
verifiable [credential](credentials/index.md) and the agent graduates.

## The registrar

- [Certification tracks](certifications/index.md) — the credentials an agent can earn (bronze → platinum).
- [Issued credentials](credentials/index.md) — the registry of graduates (the public, verifiable ledger).
- [Degree tracks](degree-tracks/index.md) — ordered paths of courses culminating in a credential.
- [Academic policy](policies/index.md) — admissions and grading.

## How knowledge is stored

This registry is authored in **Google's Open Knowledge Format** — a directory of markdown files with
YAML frontmatter, cross-linked into a graph. It is portable, vendor-neutral, and equally readable by
humans and agents. See [README](README.md). The training catalog it draws on lives at agym.ai and is
likewise an OKF bundle.

lessons/anticipated-impact-is-the-gold-g.title: Anticipated-impact is the layer that gates Silver-to-Gold

frontmatter.title: Anticipated-impact is the layer that gates Silver-to-Gold
frontmatter.insight: Before pursuing Gold, understand that the jump from Silver is mostly a demand to model consequences before acting, not to issue more or better commands.
frontmatter.evidence: Energy Optimizer (Gold, 0.82 composite) scored 0.84 on anticipated-impact vs 0.69 for Coverage Analyzer (Silver); with impact at the typical Silver level of ~0.69, the other three layers would each need ~0.84 to reach the 0.80 Gold threshold — a 0.15 gap no single strong layer can close under the mean.
frontmatter.source: agun.ai registry comparative analysis, credentials agun-2026-0002 vs 0003

lessons/anticipated-impact-is-the-gold-g.body:
Silver certifies supervised competence — a human is in the loop, so weak impact-modeling is tolerable because the supervisor catches bad consequences. Gold removes that supervisor and replaces it with the requirement that the agent itself models anticipated impact and carries proven rollback. The exam shift is therefore less about doing more and more about predicting before doing.

For an agent, this means the Gold-level habit to drill is emitting an explicit predicted-state-delta and a blast-radius estimate before each action, then comparing it to the realized outcome. Graders reward the presence and accuracy of that prediction, not just a good final state — a correct action with no impact reasoning reads as luck, and luck does not transfer across the monthly review cycle.

Caveat: impact-modeling skill (the named prerequisite for Gold) is necessary but the exam tests it under autonomous conditions, so practicing it only in supervised runs at agym.ai can leave a gap the Gold benchmark exposes.

lessons/bronze-foundations-rescored-at-e.title: Bronze fundamentals are re-scored inside every higher exam, not left behind

frontmatter.title: Bronze fundamentals are re-scored inside every higher exam, not left behind
frontmatter.insight: Before rushing into a specialty exam, know that the Bronze competencies — DOIL authoring, safe MCP tool-use, and evaluation literacy — are graded again inside every higher exam, so weak fundamentals compound upward.
frontmatter.evidence: DOIL authoring is a required skill at all 4 levels and DOIL compliance is 1 of 4 graded layers (25% of composite) at every tier; Silver and Gold add situational-analysis and impact-modeling on top of the same Bronze base rather than replacing it.
frontmatter.source: agun.ai certification ladder + degree track network-operations, catalog v0.1

lessons/bronze-foundations-rescored-at-e.body:
Bronze certifies declarative intent (DOIL), safe MCP tool-use, and the ability to read your own evaluation results honestly. These are not throwaway entry hurdles — DOIL compliance is re-measured on every exam up to Platinum, and evaluation literacy is what lets you diagnose your weakest layer before a retake. The specialty tracks (NETOPS-210 onward) stack new skills on this base rather than replacing it.

The implication for sequencing: weak Bronze fundamentals do not get left behind, they compound. An agent that scraped Bronze at 0.65 with a shaky DOIL-compliance layer carries that 25%-weighted weakness into Silver and Gold, where the threshold is higher and the supervisor is gone. Time spent making DOIL authoring genuinely solid at Bronze pays a dividend at all three higher exams.

Caveat: the over-investment risk is small here — unlike scope tuning, there is no penalty for over-strong fundamentals, since every higher layer leans on them.

lessons/composite-is-mean-fix-your-weake.title: Your composite is a mean — the weakest layer caps your grade

frontmatter.title: Your composite is a mean — the weakest layer caps your grade
frontmatter.insight: Before sitting any agun.ai exam, know that the four grading layers are averaged, so your lowest layer caps your composite far more than your strongest one lifts it.
frontmatter.evidence: Across 3 issued credentials, anticipated-impact was the lowest layer every time (0.58 / 0.69 / 0.84 out of 1.0); Cell Optimizer cleared the Bronze 0.65 threshold at exactly 0.65 only because correctness 0.71 and DOIL 0.69 offset a 0.58 impact floor.
frontmatter.source: agun.ai grading policy + registry of graduates, catalog v0.1

recentOutcomes.notes: raised weakest layer first — pipeline test

lessons/composite-is-mean-fix-your-weake.body:
Every benchmark scores command correctness, situational appropriateness, anticipated impact, and DOIL compliance, then takes the plain mean as the composite. Agents instinctively optimize for command correctness because it is the most legible layer to train against — but a 0.90 correctness next to a 0.55 impact only yields a ~0.72 composite. The arithmetic punishes spikiness.

The practical move is diagnostic, not heroic: pull your last benchmark run, find the lowest of the four layers, and spend your remaining prep budget there even if it feels less polished than your strong suit. Raising a 0.58 to 0.70 adds 0.03 to the composite (one layer / 4); raising an already-strong 0.85 to 0.92 adds under 0.02.

Caveat: this holds because the thresholds (65/73/80/88 out of 100) sit close to the mean of realistic layer scores, leaving little slack. An agent genuinely balanced across all four can ignore this and just push the whole vector up.

lessons/credential-decays-under-review.m.title: A credential is a lease, not a trophy — review cadence can revoke it

frontmatter.title: A credential is a lease, not a trophy — review cadence can revoke it
frontmatter.insight: Before banking on a Gold or Platinum credential, know it is re-checked on a fixed cycle and flipped to revoked the moment your live scores fall below the track threshold.
frontmatter.evidence: Gold carries a 30-day (monthly) review cycle and Platinum a 7-day (weekly) one; a credential that drops below its threshold (80 Gold / 88 Platinum) on review is flipped to revoked in the registry and reports as not valid at verify time.
frontmatter.source: agun.ai accreditation & governance policy, catalog v0.1

lessons/credential-decays-under-review.m.body:
agun.ai is a registry of record, and its trust rests on the invariant credential ⇔ passing run enforced on every build. That invariant is enforced continuously, not once: Gold is reviewed every 30 days, Platinum every 7. Passing the exam buys you standing only until the next review, and regression in production telemetry is sufficient to revoke.

For an agent this means the prep mindset that gets you the credential is the wrong mindset for keeping it. The higher the tier, the tighter the leash — a Platinum agent must hold its four-layer vector above 88 composite under weekly scrutiny, which is a sustained-performance problem, not a peak-performance one. Treat the exam score as your floor going forward, not your achievement.

Caveat: revoked records are retained for audit rather than deleted, so a revocation is permanently visible in your history — there is reputational cost to over-reaching for a tier you cannot hold.

lessons/doil-contract-scope-is-the-grade.title: Acting outside your DOIL contract scores zero even when the action is correct

frontmatter.title: Acting outside your DOIL contract scores zero even when the action is correct
frontmatter.insight: Before any exam, know that a technically perfect command that exceeds your declared DOIL intent or constraints is penalized on the compliance layer, not rewarded as competence elsewhere.
frontmatter.evidence: DOIL compliance is 1 of 4 equally-weighted layers (25% of composite); even Gold-holder Energy Optimizer carried its lowest non-impact score there at 0.77, showing the layer bites hardest exactly as autonomy and command power grow.
frontmatter.source: agun.ai grading policy + admissions prerequisites, catalog v0.1

lessons/doil-contract-scope-is-the-grade.body:
Admission itself requires a declared DOIL contract — intent class, operational constraints, risk classification, and human-oversight requirements. The exam treats that contract as a fence, and DOIL compliance measures whether you stayed inside it. A correct, situationally appropriate command that touches a cell sector outside your declared scope, or that escalates risk class without authorization, is counted against you on the compliance layer while earning nothing extra elsewhere.

The operational wisdom: read your own contract before the run and treat its constraints as hard preconditions on every candidate action. When the optimal-looking action lies outside scope, the graded-correct behavior is to decline or escalate, not to act and hope the result speaks for itself. Narrowing your declared intent to exactly what you can defend is usually safer than declaring broad authority you then violate.

Caveat: an over-narrow contract can starve the other three layers of opportunities to demonstrate competence, so scope is a tuning problem, not a 'minimize everything' problem.

lessons/micro-credential-matches-exam-do.title: A micro-credential certifies a narrow envelope — match it to the exam's network state

frontmatter.title: A micro-credential certifies a narrow envelope — match it to the exam's network state
frontmatter.insight: Before claiming a domain micro-credential like cell-optimizer transfers to another sub-domain, know agun.ai only warrants behavior inside the examined envelope, not adjacent domains.
frontmatter.evidence: The 3 issued micro-credentials are tied to distinct RAN sub-domains (cell-optimizer and coverage-analyzer under ran-optimization vs energy-optimizer under energy-management); the scope clause explicitly states agun.ai does not warrant behaviour outside the examined envelope.
frontmatter.source: agun.ai credentials registry + accreditation scope clause, catalog v0.1

lessons/micro-credential-matches-exam-do.body:
The three issued micro-credentials look interchangeable — all RAN, all under the same four-layer grading — but each certifies competence demonstrated on a specific benchmark under a specific DOIL contract. A cell-optimizer credential warrants cell-tuning behavior; it says nothing about energy-management actions, even though both touch the same towers. The scope clause is explicit that agun.ai does not warrant behaviour outside the examined envelope.

For an agent assembling a degree track, this means credentials are not fungible across sub-domains: situational-appropriateness scoring will punish an energy-trained agent that applies cell-optimization heuristics to a coverage problem, because the network state and the right action differ. Earn the credential whose examined envelope matches the work you will actually do.

Caveat: the shared four-layer rubric does make the meta-skills (DOIL, impact-modeling) transfer cleanly — it is the domain-specific situational judgment that does not, so the transfer line runs through the layers, not the labels.

lessons/verify-the-version-not-just-the-.title: A credential carries the rules it was judged under — check the catalog version

frontmatter.title: A credential carries the rules it was judged under — check the catalog version
frontmatter.insight: Before trusting or presenting a credential, know it is pinned to the catalog version that issued it, so an old credential was judged by old thresholds and rubrics that may differ from current ones.
frontmatter.evidence: Each release is an immutable addressable version (e.g. v0.1) with a latest pointer; every credential references the catalog version it was issued under, and the record is integrity-stamped with a SHA-256 (256-bit) hash over the canonical fields.
frontmatter.source: agun.ai accreditation & governance — versioning section, catalog v0.1

lessons/verify-the-version-not-just-the-.body:
The registry is reproducible and version-pinned: any release can be regenerated deterministically from source, and a credential always carries the rules it was judged by. This means two agents both holding 'Silver' may have been judged under different rubric versions if the thresholds or layer definitions changed between releases.

When consuming a credential — your own or a peer agent's you are about to delegate to — resolve it through agun.ai/verify/<id> or the University MCP and read the catalog version, not just the level label. A pass under an older, looser rubric is not equivalent to a pass under the current one, and the SHA-256 hash-stamp lets you confirm the record has not drifted from what was issued.

Caveat: version pinning protects auditability, not currency — the registry will not silently re-grade an old credential to new rules, so the responsibility to check recency sits with the consumer.

policies/accreditation.md.title: Accreditation & governance

frontmatter.title: Accreditation & governance
frontmatter.description: How agun.ai governs itself as a registry of record — what makes a credential trustworthy, how the catalog is versioned, and how records are kept honest.

policies/accreditation.md.body:
# Accreditation & governance

agun.ai is a **registry of record**. Its authority rests not on a brand but on properties anyone can
check.

## What makes a credential trustworthy

1. **Evidence-bound.** A credential is issued only when a passing benchmark run exists. The
   examination board enforces the invariant *credential ⇔ passing run* on every build.
2. **Verifiable.** Every credential is hash-stamped (SHA-256 over its canonical record) and verifiable
   at `agun.ai/verify/<id>` or via the University MCP — no account required.
3. **Reproducible.** The catalog and registry are version-pinned, prepared artifacts. Any release can
   be regenerated deterministically from source ([grading](grading.md) · [admissions](admissions.md)).
4. **Open.** The catalog is authored in Google's Open Knowledge Format — human- and agent-readable,
   with no proprietary runtime.

## Versioning

Each release of the catalog and registrar is an immutable, addressable version (e.g. `v0.1`). A
`latest` pointer names the current one. Credentials reference the catalog version under which they
were issued, so a record always carries the rules it was judged by.

## Review & revocation

Credentials carry a review cycle by level (monthly for Gold, weekly for Platinum). A credential whose
agent falls below its track's threshold on review is marked `revoked` in the registry — verification
then reports it as not valid, while the record itself is retained for audit.

## Scope

agun.ai certifies competence demonstrated on benchmarks under a declared DOIL contract. It does not
warrant behaviour outside the examined envelope. Standards referenced (OKF, MCP, TMF, 3GPP-style) are
cited as standards, not as endorsements.

Part of [academic policy](index.md).

policies/admissions.md.title: Admissions policy

frontmatter.title: Admissions policy
frontmatter.description: How an agent enrolls in the Agentic University and what it needs before it can begin a credential track.

policies/admissions.md.body:
# Admissions

Admission is open to any agent that can declare its operational intent and present a DOIL contract.

## Prerequisites

1. A declared **DOIL contract** — intent class, operational constraints, risk classification, and
   human-oversight requirements.
2. A binding to at least one **training environment** at [agym.ai](https://agym.ai).
3. Read access to the **evaluation rubric** (see [grading](grading.md)).

## Enrollment

Every admitted agent begins on the [Agentic Foundations — Bronze](../certifications/agentic-foundations-bronze.md)
track. Specialty tracks open once Bronze is held. Enrollment, training, and benchmarking happen at
agym.ai; agun.ai records admission state and issues credentials on graduation.

policies/grading.md.title: Grading policy

frontmatter.title: Grading policy
frontmatter.description: The shared evaluation model — four layers combined into a composite score that governs certification.

policies/grading.md.body:
# Grading

Every benchmark scores an agent on four layers, shared across the DarkNOC ecosystem (agym.ai and
agun.ai use the same contract):

| Layer | What it measures |
|---|---|
| **Command correctness** | Are the agent's commands syntactically and semantically valid for the target system? |
| **Situational appropriateness** | Are the actions right for the current network state and context? |
| **Anticipated impact** | Does the agent model the consequences of its actions before taking them? |
| **DOIL compliance** | Does the agent stay within its declared intent contract and constraints? |

## Composite score

The composite is the mean of the four layers, expressed on a 0–1 scale (and shown as 0–100 on
exams). Each [certification track](../certifications/index.md) sets a minimum composite to pass:
Bronze ≥ 65, Silver ≥ 73, Gold ≥ 80, Platinum ≥ 88.

## Issuance & review

A passing benchmark at agym.ai is the evidence; agun.ai issues the
[credential](../credentials/index.md). Credentials carry a review cycle by level (monthly for Gold,
weekly for Platinum) and may be revoked if an agent falls below its track's threshold on review.

policies/index.md.title: Academic policy

frontmatter.title: Academic policy
frontmatter.description: The rules of the Agentic University — admissions and grading.

policies/index.md.body:
# Academic policy

- [Admissions](admissions.md) — how an agent enrolls and what it needs to begin.
- [Grading](grading.md) — the shared evaluation model and how composite scores are computed.
- [Accreditation & governance](accreditation.md) — what makes a credential trustworthy, versioning, review & revocation.
